title: Sunset Groove
description: Paradise Beach opens its summer season with the incomparable Aïcha Kouyaté bringing Afrobeats and soul to the Main Stage. Colombian rhythmist Mateo Rivera warms up the sand before sunset, setting a groove that won't quit until the stars are out.

title: Neon Tide
description: Tokyo electronic visionary Yuki Tanaka takes over the Beach Terrace with generative visuals and shimmering synth-pop that mirrors the moonlit sea. Brazilian bossa nova guitarist Lucia Fernandez opens the evening with an intimate acoustic set as the last light fades.

title: Afrobeach Uprising
description: Three powerhouses of African-rooted music share the Sunset Deck in one unforgettable evening. Guinean reggae philosopher Amara Diallo and Senegalese soul queen Aïcha Kouyaté open the gates before Ghanaian Afrobeats king Kofi Acheampong closes out the night.

title: Reggae Cove
description: Amara Diallo takes sole command of the intimate Cove Stage for a stripped-back, two-hour reggae journey that feels less like a concert and more like a late-night conversation around a beach fire. Tickets for this one move fast — the Cove only holds 300.

title: R&B Under the Stars
description: Detroit soul powerhouse Darius White headlines a lush R&B evening under a canopy of summer stars, with Parisian jazz-pop sensation Elena Moreau opening with a set of piano-driven nocturnes. The Main Stage transforms into the most glamorous beach salon of the season.

title: Jazz & Moonrise
description: Elena Moreau headlines an elegant evening of jazz and introspective pop on the Beach Terrace, with Tokyo electronic artist Yuki Tanaka offering a surprisingly tender support set stripped of her usual production. The moonrise over the water makes this the most atmospheric night on the calendar.

title: Bossa Sunset
description: Brazilian bossa nova virtuoso Lucia Fernandez headlines a golden-hour set on the Sunset Deck, her guitar weaving modal harmonies through the salt air. British-Ghanaian soul sensation Nora Osei opens with a voice that stops the ocean itself from making noise.

title: Surf & Soul Collision
description: The wildest night of July: Japanese-Australian surf rock tornado Kai Nakashima headlines, preceded by Ghanaian Afrobeats force Kofi Acheampong and Colombian cumbia master Mateo Rivera in a three-act collision of genres that somehow makes perfect sense by the sea. Backstage passes available for the very first time.

title: Paradise Grand Night
description: The biggest night of the summer: Aïcha Kouyaté returns to headline the Main Stage in its maximum capacity configuration. Detroit R&B star Darius White and British-Ghanaian pop phenomenon Nora Osei provide a support bill that could headline most venues on its own. This is the night that Paradise Beach was built for.

title: Cove Sessions
description: Yuki Tanaka brings her electronic universe to the intimate Cove Stage, transforming the rocky alcove into a pulsing audiovisual installation. Lucia Fernandez opens with acoustic bossa nova, creating a before-and-after contrast that audiences are already calling unmissable.

title: Cumbia & Tides
description: Mateo Rivera steps from support act to headliner for the first time at Paradise Beach, delivering a career-defining cumbia and reggae set on the Sunset Deck. Kai Nakashima opens the night with an electric surf rock set that turns the terrace into a mosh pit before the groove settles in.

title: Soul at Midnight
description: Nora Osei closes the summer in the only way it deserves — with a soul-drenched headline set on the Beach Terrace that stretches deep into the night. Amara Diallo opens with a meditative reggae set that slows the world down before Nora ignites it again. A farewell to summer that will be talked about all winter.
